posts.name: R Davis
posts.cooked:
What Bouchard comes wrapped in black tissue with the letters “UR” below the Roses de Jeanne?
Thanks
Bob

posts.name: D@vid K@ntr0witz
posts.cooked: U R not very bright if you follow his wines and can’t figure this out

posts.name: Zac M1n0r
posts.cooked:
They changed their labels with a recent vintage, and they’re quite challenging to read at a glance now.

UR = Les Ursules
VV = Vil Vilaine
BE = Bechalin

posts.name: Mar.kus Meer
posts.cooked: Wow, are the letters the only identifier? Nothing on the back?

posts.name: Andrew Northup
posts.cooked:
Yeah the new labels only have the code and the disgorgement date. Current releases

BO - 2017 Boloree
PR - 2017 Presle
BE - 2014 Bechalin
VV - 2019 Val Vilaine
UR - 2017 Ursules
CE R - 2017 Creux d’Enfer

posts.name: Michael S. Monie
posts.cooked: The labels sucked before the change!

posts.name: Andrew Northup
posts.cooked:
The labels sucked before the change!

I like the new ones, the color differentiation is easier to read and I can still tell which wine is which just as easily

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked: My understanding from talking to people in Champagne is that it was done partially to make the new labels more Instagram friendly.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
My understanding from talking to people in Champagne is that it was done partially to make the new labels more Instagram friendly.

Since neither Cédric nor his wife are, to my knowledge, on Instagram, I think that’s a somewhat implausible as well as an uncharitable assumption.

Cédric told me that they decided to stop using wooden cases and took the opportunity to refresh the labelling as well.

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked:
My understanding from talking to people in Champagne is that it was done partially to make the new labels more Instagram friendly.

Since neither Cédric nor his wife are, to my knowledge, on Instagram, I think that’s a somewhat implausible as well as an uncharitable assumption.

Cédric told me that they decided to stop using wooden cases and took the opportunity to refresh the labelling as well.

I did not mean it to be uncharitable in the slightest! I’m on Instagram. But if it’s wrong, very happy to have the record corrected - merely repeating what I’d heard.
Thanks as always for the information William.

posts.name: Michael S. Monie
posts.cooked:
My understanding from talking to people in Champagne is that it was done partially to make the new labels more Instagram friendly.

Since neither Cédric nor his wife are, to my knowledge, on Instagram, I think that’s a somewhat implausible as well as an uncharitable assumption.

Cédric told me that they decided to stop using wooden cases and took the opportunity to refresh the labelling as well.

Does he know that paper boxes are made from trees also?

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
My understanding from talking to people in Champagne is that it was done partially to make the new labels more Instagram friendly.

Since neither Cédric nor his wife are, to my knowledge, on Instagram, I think that’s a somewhat implausible as well as an uncharitable assumption.

Cédric told me that they decided to stop using wooden cases and took the opportunity to refresh the labelling as well.

Does he know that paper boxes are made from trees also?

It isn’t a subject that gets me particularly hot under the collar, as in the final analysis it’s trivial compared to the environmental impact of many of our daily activities, but it’s a little fatuous to suggest any real equivalence between recycled cardboard and heavy wooden boxes.

The deciding factor, though, was the fact that apparently most of his importers immediately open the cases, remove the bottles, and dump the cases, as very few end-consumers get a 12-bottle allocation.

For anyone who didn’t ever see them, the OWCs were really beautifully done, so I’m actually sorry to see them go, but that’s how it is.

posts.name: Michael S. Monie
posts.cooked: I have several of his wooden boxes and have always appreciated the fact that his premium product was packaged in a premium container. I’ll be sure to hang onto them as they may one day be a collector’s item.

posts.name: M. Ch@o
posts.cooked:
It isn’t a subject that gets me particularly hot under the collar, as in the final analysis it’s trivial compared to the environmental impact of many of our daily activities, but it’s a little fatuous to suggest any real equivalence between recycled cardboard and heavy wooden boxes.

The deciding factor, though, was the fact that apparently most of his importers immediately open the cases, remove the bottles, and dump the cases, as very few end-consumers get a 12-bottle allocation.

For anyone who didn’t ever see them, the OWCs were really beautifully done, so I’m actually sorry to see them go, but that’s how it is.

I also understand they have gotten significantly more expensive in the last year or so.
